name: When Do Multi-Agent Systems Help? An Information Bottleneck Perspective
summary: arXiv:2607.16133v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: LLM powered multi-agent systems (MAS) have emerged as a promising paradigm for complex tasks. However, their advantages over single-agent systems (...
description: arXiv:2607.16133v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: LLM powered multi-agent systems (MAS) have emerged as a promising paradigm for complex tasks. However, their advantages over single-agent systems (SAS) remain unclear, with performance varying inconsistently across settings. Here, we provide an information bottleneck perspective on elucidating the differences between MAS and SAS. Specifically, o...
author: Wendi Yu, Lianhao Zhou, Xiangjue Dong, Sai Sudarshan Barath, Declan Staunton, Byung-Jun Yoon, Xiaoning Qian, James Caverlee, Shuiwang Ji
